title: Swida macrophylla
intro: Swida macrophylla, also known as Cornus macrophylla, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the family Cornaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Swida macrophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Swida macrophylla is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional Chinese patent medicines, where it is valued for its ability to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Swida macrophylla belongs to the family of plants known as Adoxaceae, which includes other shrub species such as Viburnum. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower arrangement. Within the Adoxaceae family, Swida macrophylla is part of the genus Swida, which is a group of deciduous shrubs native to East Asia. The genus Swida is further divided into multiple species, with Swida macrophylla being one of the larger species in terms of leaf size. The classification of Swida macrophylla is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.
uses: Swida macrophylla, also known as the bigleaf elderberry, has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its berries, leaves, and flowers are used in various remedies, often to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The plant's berries are rich in antioxidants, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Swida macrophylla may have antiviral properties, making it a potential treatment for certain viral infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

constituents: Swida macrophylla, also known as bigleaf huckleberry, contains various active constituents including ellagic acid, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
